# Abrahams Solicitors

> UK immigration, housing disrepair, and personal injury solicitors.
> Fixed fees, direct solicitor access, and a 95% first-time approval rate
> on immigration applications. Authorised and regulated by the Solicitors
> Regulation Authority (SRA firm #809071).

Offices in London (EC2V 8AU) and Bradford (BD7 1HR). Video consultations
available UK-wide. Free 30-minute consultation with a qualified solicitor,
no obligation.

Contact: 0203 355 9823 · info@abrahamssolicitors.co.uk
Opening hours: Mon–Fri 9:00am – 5:00pm · New enquiries monitored 24 hours

## Core services

- [UK Spouse Visa Solicitors](https://www.abrahamssolicitors.co.uk/v6/uk-spouse-visa-solicitors/): Fixed-fee spouse visa applications from £900. Handles the £29,000 income requirement, relationship evidence, and Home Office submission.
- [British Citizenship](https://www.abrahamssolicitors.co.uk/v6/british-citizenship-solicitors/): Naturalisation applications from £600. Life in the UK Test guidance and English language evidence support.
- [Indefinite Leave to Remain (ILR)](https://www.abrahamssolicitors.co.uk/v6/indefinite-leave-to-remain-ilr/): ILR applications from £750 with a 96% approval rate.
- [Sponsor Licence Applications](https://www.abrahamssolicitors.co.uk/v6/sponsor-licence-applications/): Worker, Student, and Temporary Work sponsor licences for UK employers.
- [Visa Extensions & Renewals](https://www.abrahamssolicitors.co.uk/v6/uk-visa-extensions-renewals/): Fixed-fee visa extensions across spouse, work, family, and student categories.
- [Asylum Applications](https://www.abrahamssolicitors.co.uk/v6/asylum-applications/): Legal Aid / fixed-fee asylum and human rights applications.
- [Housing Disrepair Claims](https://www.abrahamssolicitors.co.uk/v6/housing-disrepair-claims/): No Win, No Fee claims against landlords for damp, mould, heating failures, and unsafe conditions.
- [Visa Refusal Appeals](https://www.abrahamssolicitors.co.uk/v6/visa-refusal-appeal/): Administrative reviews and tribunal appeals from £1,250.

## Housing Disrepair Claims

Specialist housing disrepair solicitor: Sannah Khatoon (SRA #654258, admitted 2018).
Verify on SRA register: https://www.sra.org.uk/consumers/register/person/?sraNumber=654258

Definition: Housing disrepair is when a landlord (council, housing association, or
private) fails to keep a rented home in repair after the tenant has given notice.
The legal duty arises from Section 11 of the Landlord and Tenant Act 1985 and
Section 9A of the Homes (Fitness for Human Habitation) Act 2018.

Coverage: Council tenants, housing association tenants, and private landlord tenants
across England and Wales. Common claim types: damp and mould, leaks (roof/pipes),
broken heating and hot water, faulty wiring and electrics, structural disrepair
(cracks, plaster, subsidence), pest infestations, drainage failures.

Funding: No Win, No Fee (Conditional Fee Agreement) for most clients. Success fee
capped at 25% of damages. After-the-Event insurance covers opponent's costs if the
claim is unsuccessful. Legal Aid eligibility checked first — if you qualify, the
case runs at no cost to you.

Time limits: Six years from the date the disrepair began (Limitation Act 1980).
Three years from the date of awareness if there is a personal injury element.

Outcomes (citable, source: Abrahams internal case management data Jan 2024 – Dec 2025,
available on request): 91% of 323 housing disrepair cases concluded in this period
settled with damages. Typical damages range £1,500 – £15,000 depending on severity
and duration. Past results do not guarantee future outcomes.

Process: Pre-Action Protocol for Housing Conditions Claims (England). Steps —
(1) free 30-minute case assessment, (2) letter of claim served on landlord with
20-working-day response window, (3) independent chartered surveyor inspection
producing a Schedule of Works, (4) negotiated settlement or court proceedings
including injunction forcing repairs. Most cases settle within 6 to 12 months
without a court hearing.

Comparison with Housing Ombudsman: The Housing Ombudsman is a free service that
investigates complaints against social landlords (council/HA only) but cannot
order repairs to be carried out and average compensation is around £600. A
solicitor-led court claim under Section 11 secures binding repairs and damages
typically of £1,500 – £15,000+, and applies to private landlords as well.

Page: https://www.abrahamssolicitors.co.uk/v6/housing-disrepair-claims/
Telephone for housing disrepair: 0203 355 9823
